description: Baseline canary recorded: /msp/settings partial size + ACTIONS_MODULE count + per-package breakdown (expect ~31.85MB / 237); also settings/sla and settings/notifications sub-route partials
Tier 1: UserManagement.tsx:61 QuickAddContact -> @alga-psa/clients/components/contacts/QuickAddContact (kills the clients components barrel, top offender)
Tier 1: TicketingSettings.tsx:8 BoardsSettings/CategoriesSettings/DisplaySettings -> granular @alga-psa/tickets/components/settings/* files (kills the tickets components barrel)
Tier 1: SettingsPage.tsx:81 ProjectSettings -> @alga-psa/projects/components/settings/ProjectSettings; :68 TimeEntrySettings -> granular scheduling settings path
Tier 1: SettingsPage.tsx:69 billing (BillingSettings, TaxDelegationNudge, QboSyncHealthPanel, QboOnboardingWizardEntry) -> granular billing settings component files
Tier 1: SettingsPage.tsx:72,79 integrations (IntegrationsSettingsPage, EmailProviderConfiguration) -> granular integrations settings/email paths

Tier 1: NotificationsTab.tsx:9 (4 notification settings components), TicketingSettings.tsx:9 + InteractionStatusSettings.tsx:23,25 (reference-data components), ClientPortalSettings.tsx:26 useBranding (tenancy components) -> granular files

Tier 2: clients/actions barrel at 5 sites (GeneralSettings:14, UserManagement:10,11, InteractionTypeSettings:8-12, QuickAddInteractionType:11) -> queryActions / contact-actions/contactActions / interactionTypeActions

Tier 2: user-composition/actions barrel at 9 sites (UserManagement, UserList, UserDetails, TeamList, TeamDetails, ClientPortalSettings, InteractionStatusSettings, OrgChart) -> userQueryActions / avatarActions

Tier 2: users/actions barrel at 4 sites (UserManagement:8, UserList:10, UserDetails:6, CollapsiblePasswordChangeForm:10) -> user-actions/userActions + userClientActions; resolve role-fn defining file (OQ2)
Tier 2: teams/actions barrel at 3 sites (TeamManagement:6, TeamList:4-5, TeamDetails:9-12) -> team-actions/teamActions + team-actions/avatarActions

Tier 2: tenancy/actions barrel at 8 sites (GeneralSettings, MspLanguageSettings, ClientPortalSettings x3, ExperimentalFeaturesSettings, SecretsManagement, SecretDialog) -> coreTenantActions / tenantSettingsActions / tenantLocaleActions / tenantBrandingActions / tenantClientPortalLocaleActions / portalDomainActions / tenant-secret-actions (verify flagged ones)

Tier 2: reference-data/actions at 4 sites (ChecklistTemplatesSettings:30, InteractionStatusSettings:8,9, InteractionTypeSettings:13) -> priorityActions / status-actions/statusActions / referenceDataActions

Tier 2: client-portal/actions (UserManagement:12,14 -> portalInvitationActions / clientPortalLinkActions), licensing/actions (UserManagement:54, UserDetails:17 -> license-actions), sla/actions (TicketingSettings:10 -> slaActions)

Tier 3: sweep packages/*/src/components/settings/** subtrees reached from the hub for barrel imports (known: tickets BoardsSettings.tsx:25 tickets/actions + reference-data/user-composition/teams barrels; billing BillingSettings.tsx:10 + projects ProjectSettings.tsx:6 reference-data/components) and granularize
Tier 3: canary after sweep; if one package still dominates the settings manifest, extend the sweep to that package's remaining settings-reachable components (measure-driven, no blind expansion)
Sub-routes: settings/sla/page.tsx (sla components+actions, reference-data boardActions, clients queryActions) and settings/notifications/page.tsx (notifications components) -> granular
ESLint guard extended with the three settings globs (server settings app tree, server settings components tree, packages/*/src/components/settings) - each added only when violation-free
Final gates: typecheck exit 0; guard 0 violations; /msp/settings canary target met or honest residual recorded (AC1/AC4); SCRATCHPAD results table filled
Runtime smoke: all settings tabs render; user/team/interaction-type CRUD flows work; sla + notifications sub-pages render
OQ1 decision note: if a single tab's package still dominates post-narrowing, record measurements + flag tab->segment promotion as a separate proposal (do NOT implement)
